name: Laguna de Bezas and Balsa del Pinar – circular from Rubiales
name_original: Laguna de Bezas y Balsa del Pinar – circular desde Rubiales
region_intro: Hiking around Rubiales offers diverse landscapes and accessible trails in the Teruel province of Spain. The region features varied terrain, including the unique canyon formations of the Clays of Teruel and the protected landscapes of the Sierra de Albarracín, home to Laguna de Bezas. Hikers can explore pine forests, river paths along the Guadalaviar, and areas with notable rock formations. Best hiking trails around Rubiales The most popular hiking route is Mirador Peña El Hierro – circular through the Pinares de Rodeno, an easy 2.9 miles (4.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 18 minutes to complete. This circular route leads through the distinctive Pinares de Rodeno. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peña de la Cruz – circular by the Pinares de Rodeno, a moderate 7.4 miles (11.9 km) path. This route offers views within the Pinares de Rodeno, featuring varied terrain and elevation changes. Local hikers also love the Laguna de Bezas and Balsa del Pinar – circular from Rubiales, a 9.3 miles (15.0 km) trail leading through the protected landscape of Sierra de Albarracín, often completed in about 4 hours. Hiking around Rubiales is defined by pine forests, river valleys, and unique geological formations like the Clays of Teruel. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging treks. The routes in Rubiales are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 90 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Rubiales's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Rubiales?
A: Rubiales offers a diverse network of over 15 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find everything from easy strolls to more challenging treks, allowing you to explore the region's unique landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails in Rubiales?
A: The terrain around Rubiales is quite varied. You'll encounter pine forests, river paths along the Guadalaviar, and distinctive geological formations like the canyons of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Clays of Teruel&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979738329/mirador-pena-el-hierro-circular-through-the-pinares-de-rodeno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Peña El Hierro – circular through the Pinares de Rodeno&lt;/a&gt;, lead through the unique Pinares de Rodeno, known for its striking rock formations.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Rubiales?
A: Yes, Rubiales features several excellent circular routes. For an easy option, consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979738329/mirador-pena-el-hierro-circular-through-the-pinares-de-rodeno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Peña El Hierro – circular through the Pinares de Rodeno&lt;/a&gt;. A moderate, longer circular path is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976369919/pena-de-la-cruz-circular-by-the-pinares-de-rodeno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de la Cruz – circular by the Pinares de Rodeno&lt;/a&gt;, offering varied terrain and elevation changes within the Pinares de Rodeno. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976361402/laguna-de-bezas-and-balsa-del-pinar-circular-from-rubiales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Bezas and Balsa del Pinar – circular from Rubiales&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking near Rubiales?
A: Hikers can discover several notable natural features. The dramatic canyon formations of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Clays of Teruel&lt;/a&gt; are a must-see. You can also visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/756481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Bezas&lt;/a&gt;, a significant lagoon in the protected Sierra de Albarracín, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5128467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalaviar Nature Trail&lt;/a&gt; along the river. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5376480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Cruz (Peña Botadera) Lookout&lt;/a&gt; provides scenic views.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible on hikes in the Rubiales area?
A: Yes, the region offers opportunities to see waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10374372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorges of the Ebrón River – Calicanto Waterfall loop from Tormón&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate hike that specifically features the Cascada de Calicanto, providing a beautiful natural spectacle.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Rubiales?
A: The hiking routes in Rubiales are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 90 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the unique geological formations, and the well-maintained paths that lead through pine forests and past scenic lakes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/756481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Bezas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Rubiales?
A: Yes, Rubiales has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. One such route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979738329/mirador-pena-el-hierro-circular-through-the-pinares-de-rodeno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Peña El Hierro – circular through the Pinares de Rodeno&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 4.6 km (2.9 miles) path that can be completed in just over an hour, offering a pleasant walk through distinctive landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Rubiales?
A: Rubiales offers hiking opportunities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for hiking, with vibrant flora in spring and beautiful colors in autumn. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended. Winter hiking is also possible, though some trails might be affected by weather conditions.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: For those seeking a more demanding experience, Rubiales and its surroundings offer longer trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981549377/route-live-with-the-river-from-villel-to-villaspesa-pr-te-94" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route Live with the River from Villel to Villaspesa (PR-TE 94)&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 24.5 km (15.2 miles) trail, providing a significant challenge with varied elevation changes.

Q: Can I find trails that pass by lakes or reservoirs in the Rubiales area?
A: Absolutely. Several routes allow you to enjoy waterside scenery. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976361402/laguna-de-bezas-and-balsa-del-pinar-circular-from-rubiales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Bezas and Balsa del Pinar – circular from Rubiales&lt;/a&gt; trail takes you through the protected landscape of Sierra de Albarracín, passing by &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/756481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Bezas&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4483018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsa del Pinar&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arquillo de San Blas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; nearby.
tips: The Bezas Lagoon is the largest lagoon in the Albarracín mountain range. It extends through the municipalities of Bezas and Albarracín, within the Protected Landscape of the Pinares de Rodeno. This beautiful lagoon presents lacustrine vegetation conditioned by significant fluctuations in water level. Surrounded by pleasant hills covered with Rodeno pine and juniper trees, Bezas Lagoon is a very charming place located at about 1,235 meters above sea level. If you have the opportunity to visit it, you will fall in love with its natural beauty and picturesque surroundings. 😊
A very particular wetland near the town of Rubiales. An obligatory stop on the Empty Mountains route or if you are in the area touring the beautiful landscapes and trails.
